subject: Statistics
title: Phases of Drug Testing
description: A 60-second lesson on Phases of Drug Testing.

hook.text: From lab to pharmacy shelf takes 10-15 years and costs over $1 billion. Most drugs fail along the way.

buildup.text: Phase I tests safety on a few dozen volunteers. Phase II checks if it works on a few hundred patients.

discovery.text: Phase III is the big trial: thousands of patients, randomized and controlled. This is where most drugs fail.

twist.text: 90% of drugs that enter clinical trials never reach patients. The journey is brutal and expensive.

climax.text: Phase IV monitors the drug after approval, catching rare side effects that small trials couldn't detect.

punchline.text: Four phases, a decade, and billions. That's the price of proof.

quiz.question: What is tested in Phase III of a clinical trial?
options: Effectiveness in thousands of patients through a large randomized controlled trial
Safety in a small group of healthy volunteers
Long-term side effects after the drug is on the market
